chapter_goal: Make sheaf cohomology computationally legible through resolutions, exactness tests, and de Rham comparison.
checks: toy Cech boundary squares to zero
acyclic ladder has the expected cohomology
lab: Change a gluing cocycle on a three-open cover and inspect whether it becomes a coboundary.
library_routes: NetworkX for cover and dependency graphs
pitfalls: mistaking a presheaf restriction rule for the sheaf gluing axiom
forgetting that resolutions compute only when acyclicity is available
proof_moves: replace a sheaf by a complex that is easier to compute
track kernels and images through a ladder
visual_focus: A cover nerve and a resolution ladder expose where cochains, coboundaries, and sheaf cohomology sit.
